mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates supply-side bottlenecks and fading economic momentum, which forces equity markets to price in lower future earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during early-cycle recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 4.5 percent, higher borrowing costs immediately compress equity multiples and trigger algorithmic selling, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of observations, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during periods of high mortgage rates.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rebound in industrial production following a supply shock indicates successful capacity restoration and easing input constraints, which triggers immediate equity market rallies over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds above 108 during post-shock periods fail to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation remains elevated.
